title: SatForge
description: A from-scratch CDCL SAT solver studio that grew an SMT brain. The SAT core does two-watched literals, VSIDS, first-UIP learning and restarts, emits machine-checkable DRAT proofs of UNSAT and re-verifies them in-browser with an independent RUP/RAT checker, counts models exactly (#SAT, component caching), extracts minimal unsat cores (MUS), and optimizes — a complete weighted MaxSAT engine (linear SAT-UNSAT and core-guided WPM1 over a Generalized Totalizer) plus encoders for N-Queens, Sudoku, coloring, Hamiltonian cycles, integer factoring (a multiplier circuit run backwards) and the zebra puzzle. NEW: a full DPLL(T) SMT solver built on the very same engine — SAT lifted to first-order theories. A lazy DPLL(T) loop abstracts a formula's Boolean skeleton, and on each candidate hands the theory atoms to from-scratch decision procedures: EUF (equality + uninterpreted functions) by proof-producing congruence closure with curried applications and minimal conflict explanations; linear real & integer arithmetic (QF_LRA/QF_LIA) by a general simplex (Dutertre–de Moura) over EXACT δ-rationals — no floating point anywhere — with conflict explanations and integer branch-and-bound; and mixed QF_UFLIA by Ackermann reduction so uninterpreted functions and arithmetic combine soundly without Nelson-Oppen. Ships a tolerant SMT-LIB 2 parser and an SMT Studio with a curated example library (congruence closure, strict ordering cycles, integer pigeonhole, UF+arithmetic) that shows the verdict, the model, and the exact theory lemmas the solver learned to close the search. NEWEST: a complete QF_BV BIT-VECTOR engine by EAGER BIT-BLASTING onto the very same CDCL core — every fixed-width bit-vector operation lowered to a from-scratch hardware circuit (ripple-carry adder, shift-add multiplier, restoring division, barrel shifters for shl/lshr/ashr, signed div/rem/mod, comparators, concat/extract/extend/rotate), so the SAT verdict IS the SMT verdict: sound and complete, no refinement loop. A width-checking SMT-LIB QF_BV parser (let, indexed operators, #b/#x/(_ bvN m) literals), a model decoder (hex/bin/unsigned/signed) that re-checks every model against an independent BigInt reference, and DRAT-CERTIFIED UNSAT — an unsatisfiable bit-vector encoding is re-verified by the existing independent RUP/RAT checker, so the answer is machine-checked. Examples factor integers by running an 8×8 multiplier backwards, prove the XOR-swap and the overflow-free average bit trick, and split signed from unsigned order. NEWEST: a complete QF_AX THEORY OF ARRAYS — McCarthy select/store, constant arrays, and full EXTENSIONALITY — decided without any new theory solver by REDUCING arrays to the EUF + arithmetic already present: read-over-write purification rewrites every select(store(a,i,v),j) into a fresh element pinned by the McCarthy axioms (recursing through nested writes), surviving reads become an uninterpreted function the congruence-closure engine already handles, and array equality is encoded by a Skolem witness index plus saturated-index agreement clauses (the Stump–Barrett–Dill scheme, made finite and ground), so both existing theories inherit arrays for free and QF_ALIA (arrays + integer arithmetic) just works. The SMT-LIB parser learned (Array I E) sorts, select/store and ((as const ...) v); the Studio renders the array contents the solver committed to as per-array cell tables. NEWEST: a complete QF_DT THEORY OF ALGEBRAIC DATATYPES — constructors, selectors and testers over lists, trees, pairs, enums and Nat — likewise decided with NO new theory solver, by REDUCING datatypes to the EUF + linear-integer arithmetic already present. The reduction is purely additive: it conjoins the free-term-algebra axioms (exactly-one-tester exhaustiveness + disjointness; constructor pinning is_C(C(…)) and sel_i(C(a))=a_i, which yields injectivity for free through selector congruence; the tester link is_C(t) → t = C(sel_1(t),…) instantiated to a bounded selector depth) plus the one genuinely non-EUF ingredient — ACYCLICITY BY INTEGER RANK: a fresh rank:D→Int with rank(t) > rank(child) on every constructor edge, so over a finite set of ground terms a strict > ordering is exactly 'no term is its own subterm' and the existing simplex/branch-and-bound refutes impossible cyclic values like x = cons(a,x). Datatypes thus reduce to QF_UFLIA, Ackermann-combined like UFLIA/QF_ALIA, so QF_DTLIA (datatypes with integer fields) just works. The SMT-LIB parser learned declare-datatype/declare-datatypes (mutually recursive), constructor/selector applications and the ((_ is C) t) tester. Everything is brute-force certified: 3000 random EUF formulas cross-checked against congruence enumeration, 2500 QF_LRA formulas against Fourier–Motzkin, 1200 mixed UF+arithmetic formulas against an Ackermann+FM reference, 1500 random bit-vector formulas against full assignment enumeration with every operator exhaustively checked, ~3700 random array formulas (non-extensional AND extensional) against an independent finite-model enumerator, and ~1500 random datatype formulas against an independent finite-TREE-model enumerator (every value a real constructor tree, testers reading the real root) that shares no code with the reduction. NEWEST: a complete QF_S BOUNDED THEORY OF STRINGS — the hardest classic theory (unbounded string equations are undecidable), added in the same style with NO new theory solver, by REDUCING strings (up to a length bound L) to the EUF + linear-integer arithmetic already present. Each string term becomes a length |s| (the reused str.len symbol) plus L integer code-units str.char$(s,k) — an uninterpreted String×Int→Int function, so EUF gives congruence for free and Ackermann already combines it with the simplex — with padding past the end pinned to a sentinel so a string's value IS its code-units. McCarthy-style per-operator axioms unfold str.++ (concatenation), str.at, str.substr, str.contains/prefixof/suffixof and str.indexof over the ≤L positions into ordinary eq/arith atoms; string equality is kept as an EUF atom AND tied to position-wise code-unit agreement, so '=' means VALUE equality ('ab'·'c' = 'abc'), not term identity. Because the alphabet is constrained only up to equality, the small-model property makes this sound and complete WITHIN the length bound (so x = 'a'·x is refuted by the simplex from |x| = 1 + |x|). The SMT-LIB parser learned the String sort, double-quoted literals and the str.* operators, and the Studio reconstructs each variable's solved text. Everything is brute-force certified: 3000 random EUF formulas cross-checked against congruence enumeration, 2500 QF_LRA formulas against Fourier–Motzkin, 1200 mixed UF+arithmetic formulas against an Ackermann+FM reference, 1500 random bit-vector formulas against full assignment enumeration with every operator exhaustively checked, ~3700 random array formulas (non-extensional AND extensional) against an independent finite-model enumerator, ~1500 random datatype formulas against an independent finite-TREE-model enumerator, and ~900 random bounded-string formulas against an independent concrete-string enumerator (plain JavaScript string semantics, sharing no code with the reduction). NEWEST: CRAIG INTERPOLATION and SAT-BASED UNBOUNDED MODEL CHECKING (McMillan 2003) — SatForge stops reasoning about one formula and starts PROVING WHOLE SYSTEMS SAFE FOR ALL TIME. A from-scratch PROOF-LOGGING CDCL records a resolution refutation (a DAG of partition-tagged input clauses and pivoted resolution steps, down to the empty clause) for every UNSAT, and McMillan's algorithm reads a Craig interpolant straight off it — a formula I with A⟹I, I∧B unsat, over only the vocabulary A and B share. Built on that, an INTERPOLATION-BASED MODEL CHECKER takes a finite-state transition system (Init, Trans, Bad), runs bounded model checking, and iterates the interpolant of one unrolled step into an INDUCTIVE INVARIANT that proves the bad state unreachable on every execution — or returns a concrete shortest counterexample trace. A Model Checker studio ships a gallery (a modulo-6 counter, a saturating counter that overflows, a mutual-exclusion protocol and its broken variant, a traffic-light controller, a token ring), each run live with verdict, invariant or counterexample, the interpolation search trace, AND an INDEPENDENT EXPLICIT-STATE BFS REACHABILITY ORACLE (sharing no code with the SAT/interpolation path) shown beside it; plus an Interpolation panel that computes and exhaustively verifies the interpolant of any editable A/B clause pair. Certified the project's way: the proof solver agrees with brute force AND the main engine on 1500 random CNFs (every model verified), ~500 random UNSAT partitions whose interpolants pass all three Craig properties by exhaustive enumeration, and 300 random total transition systems plus the whole gallery where the model checker must match the BFS oracle on verdict, inductive invariant AND shortest counterexample. A SECOND, INDEPENDENT PROOF RULE — simple-path k-INDUCTION — runs beside interpolation in the studio, so two independent proofs and the explicit-state oracle must all agree. NEWEST: a SOLVER LAB that turns the engine's heuristics into PARAMETERS (restarts, clause deletion, phase saving, VSIDS decay rate, and a genuinely-uniform random branching mode) and RACES the same proved-correct CDCL core against itself across a reproducible benchmark suite — random 3-SAT at the α ≈ 4.26 phase transition, pigeonhole UNSAT, graph coloring and Langford pairings — scoring the field the way the SAT Competition does: a from-scratch SVG CACTUS PLOT, a PAR-2 leaderboard, and a per-instance speed heatmap, all streamed off the main thread by a dedicated worker. It doubles as a SOUNDNESS ORACLE: because every configuration is the same complete solver with a single knob flipped, they must reach the SAME verdict on every instance they decide, so the panel that shows 'restarts make us faster' also continuously re-proves that no heuristic can make us WRONG — cross-checked in the harness where all eleven configurations match exhaustive truth-table brute force on hundreds of random CNFs. NEWEST: a from-scratch IC3 / PDR engine (PROPERTY-DIRECTED REACHABILITY, Bradley 2011) — a THIRD, independent unbounded-safety prover that, unlike interpolation, NEVER UNROLLS THE TRANSITION RELATION. It grows a ladder of over-approximating CNF frames F0=Init ⊆ F1 ⊆ … using only single-step SAT queries, blocks each reachable bad state by a recursively-discharged proof obligation, learns a blocking clause that it INDUCTIVELY GENERALIZES (Bradley's MIC — drop literals while the clause stays inductive relative to the previous frame), pushes clauses forward, and declares safety the moment two adjacent frames coincide (their conjunction is an inductive invariant); unsafety is established by a chain of obligations back to Init, with the shortest concrete counterexample materialized by an honest bounded unrolling. The Model Checker studio now shows FOUR verdicts side by side — interpolation, IC3/PDR, k-induction and the explicit-state BFS oracle, only green when all four agree — plus an IC3 proof panel with a live frame-ladder, MIC/obligation/SAT-query stats, and PDR's own machine-checked inductive invariant (discovered by a completely different route than the interpolation invariant), and a new maximal-length LFSR gallery example whose 'never latches up at zero' safety is proven by the one-clause invariant register≠0. Certified by six new assertions cross-checking PDR's verdicts, invariants and shortest counterexamples against BFS, interpolation AND k-induction on 220 random systems plus the gallery. NEWEST: KNOWLEDGE COMPILATION to smooth d-DNNF — SatForge stops merely COUNTING solutions and starts COMPILING the formula into a circuit on which a whole family of otherwise-#P-hard queries collapses to a single LINEAR-TIME pass. A from-scratch compiler records the #SAT DPLL search as a shared DAG in smooth, deterministic, Decomposable Negation Normal Form (sd-DNNF): unit propagation becomes an AND of forced-literal leaves, a vanished variable an OR(x,¬x) coin-flip, independent sub-formulas a DECOMPOSABLE AND (children share no variables), and a branch a DETERMINISTIC OR (the arms disagree on the split variable, so their model sets are disjoint) — with repeated sub-formulas CACHED and SHARED (the c2d/Dsharp idea) and unsatisfiable branches collapsed, so the circuit is a DAG, not a tree. Because the circuit is smooth+decomposable+deterministic, querying it is exact and linear: an exact BigInt MODEL COUNT, WEIGHTED MODEL COUNTING under arbitrary per-literal weights (the partition function behind exact probabilistic inference, tied to #SAT by Z = count/2ⁿ under uniform weights), and EVERY VARIABLE'S EXACT MARGINAL PROBABILITY in a SINGLE forward+backward sweep via the Darwiche arithmetic-circuit differential (w[ℓ]·∂Z/∂w[ℓ] = WMC(f∧ℓ)), the MOST-PROBABLE EXPLANATION (MPE) by a max-product pass, plus model ENUMERATION (each model produced once), independent STRUCTURAL CERTIFICATES that PROVE the compiled circuit really is sd-DNNF, and export to the standard c2d/Dsharp .nnf format. A new Compile studio tab compiles off-thread and shows the verified property badges, a circuit-stats grid, the exact count, and a weighted-inference panel whose TILT SLIDER recomputes the weighted model count, a LIVE VARIABLE-MARGINAL BAR CHART and the most-probable assignment in real time. Certified by seventeen new assertions: over 1200 random CNFs the compiled count matches #SAT and brute force, every circuit is verified smooth+decomposable+deterministic, the weighted count, the one-pass marginals and the max-product MPE match brute force (random and uniform weights), and enumeration equals the exact model set with no duplicates. NEWEST: a from-scratch QBF SOLVER for QUANTIFIED BOOLEAN FORMULAS — the canonical PSPACE-complete problem, a strict step above SAT/#SAT — decided on the very same CDCL core by COUNTEREXAMPLE-GUIDED EXPANSION (the RAReQS idea). A QBF is a two-player game between ∃ (satisfy the matrix) and ∀ (break it), and SatForge plays it ONE QUANTIFIER BLOCK AT A TIME: at each block the searching player proposes a candidate move with a SAT call over a growing set of BLOCKING clauses, checks it EXACTLY by recursing on the block-stripped subgame (the chosen move substituted into the matrix), and on failure learns the opponent's winning counter-move — ruling that candidate out — until it either wins or the SAT call goes UNSAT and the block is decided; single ∃ blocks bottom out as plain SAT, single ∀ blocks as validity. It returns a DECISIVE CERTIFICATE for the outer block (the winning ∃ assignment or the refuting ∀ assignment) plus a full per-iteration refinement trace and search stats, all under explicit budgets that yield a clean UNKNOWN instead of hanging. Ships a tolerant QDIMACS parser/serializer, scalable families whose value is KNOWN BY CONSTRUCTION (the copy game ∀ū∃ȳ.⋀yᵢ↔uᵢ that is TRUE because ∃ reacts and FALSE when the quantifiers swap because ∃ must commit; a parity ladder over an XOR-is-even matrix that is TRUE iff the innermost block is ∃), a seeded random generator, and a new QBF Studio tab — a QDIMACS editor with a live prefix/alternation readout, the verdict with its BRUTE-FORCE AGREEMENT BADGE, a VERIFIED winning-move certificate (substituted back and re-evaluated by the oracle), search-effort stats, and a colour-coded refinement trace. Certified the project's way: an independent EXHAUSTIVE Shannon-expansion oracle (∃=OR, ∀=AND) the solver must always agree with, headlined by a 1500-instance random cross-check over QBFs of 1–4 alternating blocks with ZERO mismatches, every returned witness re-verified as a genuine winning move, the copy-game and parity-ladder families at every width, a QDIMACS round-trip and determinism. NEWEST: a from-scratch BINARY DECISION DIAGRAM engine — the canonical form of a Boolean function, where two formulas are equivalent IFF they compile to the SAME node, so satisfiability, tautology and equivalence all collapse to a pointer compare. A unique-table-interned ROBDD package is built around the single universal operator ite(f,g,h) (a memoized Shannon apply): every connective, cofactor/restrict, ∃/∀ quantification, functional compose, exact BigInt model count and cube enumeration are thin wrappers over it. The headline is VARIABLE REORDERING — the lever that makes a diagram linear or astronomically large: a provably function-preserving reconstruction rebuilds the function under any order (the cofactor identity), and RUDELL SIFTING searches each variable's best level to shrink it (the bit-match function ⋁ᵢ aᵢ∧bᵢ collapses 62→10 nodes, word-equality 93→15, while parity stays 2n−1 in EVERY order). Ships a precedence-climbing Boolean-expression front-end, a gallery of order-sensitive classics, the dual ZERO-SUPPRESSED BDD (ZDD) for set families (union/intersect/difference, count, C(n,k) combinations) and a BDD Studio that draws the live diagram (solid 1-edge / dashed 0-edge) and reorders it on one click. Cross-certified from three sides: a truth-table oracle re-checks every apply, cofactor, quantifier and reorder; a BDD built from a CNF must agree with the project's OWN CDCL solver on SAT/UNSAT and with the #SAT counter on the exact model count; and the ZDD set algebra is checked against closed-form combinatorics and bit-mask set arithmetic. NEWEST: a native PSEUDO-BOOLEAN (0/1 integer-linear) engine that learns in the CUTTING-PLANE proof system — provably stronger than the resolution behind every other engine here. A pseudo-Boolean constraint is a 0/1 linear inequality Σ aᵢ·ℓᵢ ≥ d (every clause is Σ ℓᵢ ≥ 1, every cardinality constraint the unit-coefficient case); the solver propagates by SLACK (a literal whose coefficient exceeds a constraint's slack is forced; negative slack is a conflict) and analyzes conflicts by ROUNDINGSAT-STYLE GENERALIZED RESOLUTION — reduce the reason that propagated the most-recent literal (weaken its non-divisible, non-falsified literals, then Chvátal–Gomory divide-and-round so the pivot's coefficient is 1) and add it to the running conflict so the pivot cancels, repeated to a 1-UIP cut that asserts after back-jumping, with a DECISION-CUT fall-back that guarantees termination. It is SOUND BY CONSTRUCTION: every step (add, multiply by a positive integer, divide-and-round, saturate, weaken) is a valid cutting-plane inference, so a reported UNSAT is a real refutation and every SAT model is re-checked — all coefficients kept exact in BIGINT because cutting planes make them grow. The headline is the famous separation it makes visible: the PIGEONHOLE PRINCIPLE PHPⁿ⁺¹ₙ, exponentially hard for resolution/CDCL, is refuted by the native solver in a LINEAR number of conflicts — PHP(8→7) in 7 conflicts where the resolution-based CDCL core spends ~1565, shown side by side as a live bar chart. Adds 0/1 OPTIMIZATION by solution-improving (linear SAT-UNSAT) search (knapsack, minimum set cover, the Petersen graph's domination number), a tolerant OPB parser/printer, and a sixth studio that shows the verdict or proven optimum, a cross-check row (native vs. an independent CNF encoding over the project's own Generalized Totalizer + CDCL, vs. a brute-force oracle), the cutting-plane derivation of the first conflict, and a live verification button. Brute-force certified the project's way: the native solver agrees with brute force AND the CNF oracle on 4000 random instances with every model re-verified, optimization optima match the brute-force optimum, the pigeonhole family is UNSAT, and every cutting-plane rule is proven sound over thousands of random constraints. Part of 437 self-tests in all.
